L1.area: Backend administration and content management modules

L2.description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-controlled input fields are persisted to the database without proper sanitization and subsequently rendered in an administrative interface without output encoding. An attacker can inject persistent JavaScript payloads that execute automatically in the context of authenticated administrative users, enabling session hijacking, privilege escalation, and full administrative account compromise.

L3.area: Backend administration and content management modules
L3.description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-controlled input fields are persisted to the database without proper sanitization and subsequently rendered in an administrative interface without output encoding. An attacker can inject persistent JavaScript payloads that execute automatically in the context of authenticated administrative users, enabling session hijacking, privilege escalation, and full administrative account compromise.

ground_truth.reason: User-supplied name and surname fields are stored without sanitization and rendered without output encoding in the backend users listing page, allowing persistent XSS that executes in the context of authenticated administrative users.
